cards.back: Cloud computing refers to the delivery of computing services over the internet, while others define it as a model of delivering computing resources over the internet
Cloud capacity refers to the amount of resources (such as processing power, memory, and storage) available in a cloud computing environment

cards.front: Use it or Lose it (Your Wallet)
cards.back: The 'use it or lose it' principle is a reminder that unused cloud resources can lead to unnecessary costs and should be monitored and optimized
Measuring the cloud involves tracking and analyzing various metrics such as utilization, cost, and performance to optimize cloud resources

cards.front: Cloud Case Studies: Anonymous Desktop Software
cards.back: A cloud case study of Anonymous Desktop Software may involve analyzing how the company migrated its desktop applications to the cloud, and the benefits and challenges that arose from the migration
